rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $2150.00 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioners First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 15, 2013, Line 1: Petitioner PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion to Compel Answers to Petitioner's First Set of Demand for Production and Inspection of Documents; First Set of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. No Opposition filed. Claimant Rafael Carrera shall provide answers to Petitioner¿s First Set of Demand for Production and Inspection of Documents, First Set of Judicial Counsel Form Interrogatories and First set of Special Interrogatories within 15 days of the notice of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: SEIFER, MURKEN, DESPINA, JAMES & TEICHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Seifer, Murken, Despina, James & Teichman'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 15, 2013, Line 17, Petitioner Seifer, Murken, Despina, James & Teichman'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration. Granted. Pursuant to CCP 473, Petitioner's motion to amend is granted. CCP 473 is to be construed liberally to allow "an amendment to any pleading or proceeding." Respondent will not be prejudiced by the amendment. The amended pleading must be filed within 10 days of entry of this order. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., DBA GREAT VS. MICHAEL DAVID LYNDALL INDIVIDUALLY DBA LYNDALLS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Appointment Of A Receiver And To Appoint Mike Brewer As A Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 15, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC., dba GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU's Motion for Appointment of a Receiver and to Appoint Mike Brewer as a Receiver Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition was filed. The Court appoints Michael Brewer as a receiver to carry out the enforcement of the judgment in this case including including the seizure and sale of liquor license number 470741. Michael David Lyndall has 20 days from the date of this order to pay the full judgment in lieu of the seizure and sale of the liquor license. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CATALINO CARINO et al VS. ALIREZA KATOUZIAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 15, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT BRISTOL-MYERS SQUIBB COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the general demurrer to the entire First Amended Complaint ("FAC") and as to the fifth cause of action; sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action. As to the general demurrer to the entire FAC, the demurrer is overruled because Plaintiffs amended their Complaint and identified Bristol-Myers Squibb Company ("BMS") as Doe "1" as to the existing first and second causes of action. At a minimum the first and second causes of action (where BMS was identified as Doe "1") are not barred by the statute of limitations. Thus, the general demurrer to the entire First Amended Complaint on statute of limitations grounds is overruled. (See Weill & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (Rutter) 7:105.) No other grounds for demurrer are asserted as to the first and second causes of action. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff agrees to dismiss this cause of action, and the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. As to the fifth cause of action for strict product liability/failure to warn, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action. (See CACI 1205.) =(302/MJM)
